summary: Iteration 19: Multi-Branch Inventory Transfer feature fully tested. Backend: 19/19 tests passed. Frontend: All transfer workflows verified (create, approve, complete, History tab). Transfers nav correctly positioned between Stock and Kitchen. No regressions.
passed_tests: Backend: GET /stock-transfers returns list (initially empty or with data)
Backend: POST /stock-transfers creates transfer successfully with pending status
Backend: POST /stock-transfers validation - same branch returns 400 error
Backend: POST /stock-transfers validation - missing items returns 400 error
Backend: POST /stock-transfers validation - missing branches returns 400 error
Backend: PUT /stock-transfers/{id}/approve - approves pending transfer
Backend: PUT /stock-transfers/{id}/approve - already approved fails with 400
Backend: PUT /stock-transfers/{id}/complete - completes approved transfer with stock adjustment
Backend: PUT /stock-transfers/{id}/complete - cannot complete pending transfer
Backend: PUT /stock-transfers/{id}/reject - rejects pending transfer with reason
Backend: PUT /stock-transfers/{id}/reject - cannot reject already approved transfer
Backend: DELETE /stock-transfers/{id} - deletes pending transfer
Backend: DELETE /stock-transfers/{id} - deletes rejected transfer
Backend: DELETE /stock-transfers/{id} - cannot delete completed transfer (400)
Backend: Full workflow: Create → Approve → Complete with stock adjustment
Backend: Regression - Dashboard API works
Backend: Regression - Stock items API works
Backend: Regression - Branches API works
Backend: Regression - Sales API works
Frontend: /transfers page loads with 'Inventory Transfers' title
Frontend: Summary cards visible (Pending, Approved, Completed, Rejected counts)
Frontend: Tabs visible (Pending, Approved, History)
Frontend: 'New Transfer' button opens create dialog
Frontend: Create dialog has Source/Destination branch selectors, item picker, quantity, reason fields
Frontend: Created transfer appears in Pending tab with correct details
Frontend: Approve button works - transfer moves to Approved tab
Frontend: Complete Transfer button works - shows 'Transfer completed. Stock adjusted.'
Frontend: Completed transfer appears in History tab with 'completed' status badge
Frontend: Sidebar 'Transfers' nav link visible between Stock and Kitchen
Frontend: Regression - Dashboard page works
Frontend: Regression - Stock page works
Frontend: Regression - Sales page works
critical_code_review_comments: Stock Transfer workflow implemented correctly: Create(pending) → Approve(approved) → Complete(completed) with proper validation at each step
Stock adjustment on complete: Creates StockUsage (stock-out from source branch) and StockEntry (stock-in at destination branch) - tested and verified
WhatsApp notification on create silently fails if WhatsApp is not configured - acceptable behavior
Transfer cards show all required info: status badge, date, requester, FROM/TO branches, items with quantities, reason, reviewer
Validation errors properly returned: same branch, missing items, missing branches all return 400 with descriptive messages
Cannot delete completed transfers - proper protection against data loss
UI uses proper data-testid attributes for all interactive elements - good for maintainability
seed_data_creation: Created test transfers during testing (cleaned up except completed ones which cannot be deleted)
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 19 complete. Multi-Branch Inventory Transfer feature fully implemented and tested. Key endpoints: GET/POST /stock-transfers, PUT /{id}/approve, PUT /{id}/reject, PUT /{id}/complete, DELETE /{id}. Transfer workflow: Create(pending) → Approve(approved) → Complete(completed with stock adjustment). On complete, StockUsage entry created for source branch (stock-out) and StockEntry for destination (stock-in). Frontend at /transfers with tabs: Pending, Approved, History. 'Transfers' nav in sidebar between Stock and Kitchen. Branch IDs used: Test Branch=ssc-erp-demo, A=ssc-erp-demo. Item: Widget A (starting with 0d680671). All features working correctly, no regressions.
